sections.heading: S
sections.section_value: The patient reports a significant amount of pain at the end of his right middle finger, stemming from a motor vehicle accident approximately five days prior. He describes being rear-ended while stopped at a four-way stop, and an incident involving flipping someone off, which he attributes to excessive enthusiasm. He states he sustained no other injuries besides the finger.

sections.heading: O
sections.section_value: An X-ray reveals a distal phalanx fracture of the middle finger. Physical examination reveals tenderness over the distal phalanx of the right middle finger. Otherwise, the physical exam is unremarkable.
medical_concepts: distal phalanx of the right middle finger

sections.heading: A
sections.section_value: The patient's primary problem is a distal phalanx fracture of the middle finger, likely secondary to a motor vehicle accident. He is experiencing significant pain at the site of the injury.

sections.heading: P
sections.section_value: I am prescribing tramadol 50 mg every six hours as needed for pain for two days. I am also prescribing an eight-count tramadol tablet supply. A finger splint will be provided. Follow-up x-ray is scheduled in two weeks. I will counsel the patient to take the prescribed medications as directed and to monitor for any worsening of symptoms. I will also advise him to avoid activities that could further injure the finger.

medical_concepts: distal phalanx of the right middle finger
